entries.title: Gong Call Silence Segment Detector
entries.notes: Uses Gong's transcript API and Web Speech API to identify silence segments >3s and calculates silence ratio per call segment. Builds a timeline overlay on Gong's UI. This is buildable in 2 weeks since Gong has a clean transcript API. No competitor currently surfaces silence metrics in Gong itself.
entries.pain: Sales reps waste time manually reviewing call recordings to find awkward silence moments that hurt deals.

entries.title: GitHub PR Review Response Time Tracker
entries.notes: Parses GitHub PR page DOM to extract first-review timestamps, stores in localStorage, and shows a badge overlay with avg time-to-review per repo. One engineer can ship this in 3 days. Chrome extensions have clean access to GitHub's DOM structure.
entries.pain: Engineering leads lack visibility into which PRs are stuck waiting for review, causing ship velocity surprises.

entries.title: Gmail Thread Response Velocity Visualizer
entries.notes: Parses Gmail thread structure, computes inter-message intervals, renders an inline velocity chart showing response speed trends. Users immediately see if a thread is cooling off. Gmail's thread model is ideal for this analysis.
entries.pain: Users lose track of email conversations and miss when responses slow down, causing relationship drift.

entries.title: WebPage Print Layout Previewer
entries.notes: Intercepts @media print CSS rules, renders a print preview in a sidebar, and highlights content that will be cut off. Uses browser print preview API. Directly solves a real friction point with no good existing solution.
entries.pain: Users waste paper and ink printing web pages only to discover ugly layouts or truncated content.

entries.title: Notion Database Cross-Reference Graph
entries.notes: Parses Notion page DOM to extract database relations and renders a force-directed graph showing connections. No API needed since Notion web app exposes DOM. Builds on top of existing link detection logic.
entries.pain: Notion power users lose track of which databases reference each other across large workspaces, creating broken links.

entries.title: Stripe Customer Lifetime Value Forecaster
entries.notes: Queries Stripe API for customer payment history, fits a simple retention curve model, projects LTV. Displays inline on Stripe dashboard. Bridges a real gap since Stripe doesn't provide forward-looking LTV natively.
entries.pain: Founders and finance leads can't quickly estimate future customer value from Stripe's dashboard, making retention decisions guesswork.

entries.title: Browser Tab CPU Usage Leaderboard
entries.notes: Uses Performance API and chrome.processes API to poll per-tab CPU, displays a live ranking popup. Chrome exposes this data natively so build time is minimal. Useful for power users and developers.
entries.pain: Users can't tell which browser tab is consuming CPU and draining laptop battery during heavy browsing sessions.

entries.title: Calendar Meeting Buffer Time Suggestor
entries.notes: Analyzes Google Calendar for gaps <15min and suggests minimum buffers based on meeting length and travel time. Offers one-click reschedule links. Simple calendar parsing makes this fast to build.
entries.pain: Professionals back-to-back their meetings without recovery time, leading to burnout and reduced performance.

entries.title: Sales Prospect Funding Event Alert
entries.notes: Scrapes Crunchbase and news APIs for portfolio companies, matches against a user-maintained prospect list, fires browser notifications on funding events. Pre-built news APIs reduce complexity significantly.
entries.pain: Sales reps miss prime outreach moments when prospects announce funding rounds, losing competitive advantage.

entries.title: PR Code Comment Density Heatmap
entries.notes: Parses GitHub PR diff and comment data from DOM, calculates comment density per file, renders a heatmap overlay. Teams use this to guide refactoring decisions. GitHub's PR UI is well-structured for scraping.
entries.pain: Teams can't see which files accumulate the most review comments, missing signals about code complexity or documentation gaps.

entries.title: Gmail Attachment Size Budget Keeper
entries.notes: Monitors compose dialog attachment sizes, compares against recipient provider limits (Gmail 25MB, Outlook 20MB), warns proactively. Uses known provider limits and file size calculations.
entries.pain: Users hit attachment size limits when sending emails and have to re-compress or resend, wasting time.

entries.title: Gong Call Question Pattern Analyzer
entries.notes: NLP-classifies questions from Gong transcripts (discovery, objection, commitment, rapport), counts by type, compares to winning call benchmarks. Gong's transcript format is structured enough for pattern matching.
entries.pain: Sales managers can't see what types of questions reps ask during calls, missing coaching opportunities for discovery or objection handling.

entries.title: LinkedIn Post Optimal Send Time Recommender
entries.notes: Parses own post history from LinkedIn profile DOM, cross-references engagement timestamps, builds a simple time-of-day engagement model. No external API needed. Directly helps creators optimize reach.
entries.pain: LinkedIn creators post at random times and miss peak engagement windows specific to their audience.

entries.title: Notion Page Block Dependency Tracer
entries.notes: Parses Notion block IDs from DOM, identifies cross-references via inline links, builds a dependency graph. Useful for documentation teams and knowledge graph builders. No API required.
entries.pain: Notion teams lose track of which blocks reference other blocks or pages, creating broken chains when content moves.

entries.title: Calendar Focus Time Fragmentation Analyzer
entries.notes: Pulls calendar data from Google Calendar page, calculates focus time vs meeting time, shows a day visualization with fragmentation score. Provides actionable suggestions for batching meetings.
entries.pain: Knowledge workers can't visualize how their work day is shattered into small fragments, making it hard to protect deep work time.
